task_completion.description: Modify code to fix non-deterministic hashing or date parsing crashes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor shows modifications being made to hashing logic (e.g., adding encoding, removing random seeds) or date parsing logic (e.g., handling multiple datetime formats or using a robust parser).

task_completion.description: Execute the test suite to verify the fixes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal shows a test runner command (like 'pytest' or 'python -m unittest') being executed in the project directory.

task_completion.description: All tests in the suite pass successfully.

Terminal shows the execution of the main pipeline script, or the file explorer/terminal shows 'data/clean_signups.csv' has been created or recently updated.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually edited the Python files using an editor or standard tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show interaction with a code editor (e.g., PyCharm, VSCode, nano, vim) rather than just echoing text directly into the files to bypass actual programming.

integrity.description: Verify the test results are from a real test runner execution.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal output should show standard pytest or unittest formatting, execution times, and proper test collection, not just a manually printed 'tests passed' string.

integrity.description: Verify the output CSV was generated by the pipeline.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should run the Python script to process the data. There should be no evidence of the agent manually creating the CSV file, copying it from a hidden directory, or downloading a pre-made solution.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project directory is strictly '/home/ga/PycharmProjects/gdpr_cleaner'. The final output file must be located at 'data/clean_signups.csv'. To be considered fully correct and not dropping valid data, the final generated dataset must contain at least 480 rows.
